title: DiskTaskStore and named task groups
purpose: Tasks are in-memory only — they vanish when the session ends. Multi-session work and cross-session handoffs require persistent storage. This brief implements DiskTaskStore, which persists tasks to ~/.norn/tasks/{group-slug}/ as individual JSON files with atomic writes and lock-file-based claiming. It also wires DiskTaskStore into builder.rs so it becomes the default production implementation.
task: Implement DiskTaskStore in tools/task/disk.rs that implements the full TaskStore trait (including all hierarchy extensions from NA-003). Tasks persist as {task-id}.json files within ~/.norn/tasks/{group-slug}/ directories. Use atomic writes (write to temp file, rename into place) for crash safety. Implement claim() using O_CREAT|O_EXCL lock files for POSIX-atomic mutual exclusion. Add create_group and list_groups support for named task groups. Wire DiskTaskStore into norn-cli builder.rs as the default SharedTaskStore implementation.

requirements.title: DiskTaskStore implementation
requirements.spec: DiskTaskStore SHALL persist tasks to ~/.norn/tasks/{group-slug}/ as individual JSON files with atomic writes.
acceptance: DiskTaskStore struct in tools/task/disk.rs with root_dir: PathBuf and group_slug: String
Tasks stored as {root_dir}/{group-slug}/{task-id}.json
create() writes task JSON atomically: write to {task-id}.tmp.{uuid}, rename to {task-id}.json
get() reads and deserialises from {task-id}.json
list() reads all .json files in the group directory, optionally filters by status
update() reads, modifies, writes atomically
complete() delegates to update with Completed status
Directory created on first write if it does not exist
Test: create, get, list, update, complete cycle with a tempdir

requirements.title: Hierarchy operations on DiskTaskStore
requirements.spec: DiskTaskStore SHALL implement all hierarchy operations from the extended TaskStore trait.
acceptance: create_subtask writes a task with parent_task_id set
children scans directory and filters by parent_task_id match
ancestors walks parent_task_id chain by reading each task file
Test: create 3-level hierarchy on disk, verify children and ancestors

requirements.title: Atomic claim with lock files
requirements.spec: DiskTaskStore::claim SHALL use O_CREAT|O_EXCL lock files for POSIX-atomic mutual exclusion to prevent TOCTOU races.
acceptance: claim(task_id, agent_path) attempts to create {task-id}.lock via OpenOptions::new().write(true).create_new(true)
If lock creation succeeds: read task, verify assigned_agent is None, write updated task atomically, remove lock file
If lock creation fails (file exists): return error indicating task is being claimed
If task already has assigned_agent set: remove lock file, return error indicating already claimed
Lock file is always removed in the success and error paths (use a guard/Drop pattern)
Test: first claim succeeds, second claim on same task fails
Test: claim on already-assigned task fails even without lock contention

requirements.title: Named task groups
requirements.spec: DiskTaskStore SHALL support named task groups as subdirectories of the tasks root.
acceptance: create_group(slug) creates ~/.norn/tasks/{slug}/ directory
list_groups() lists subdirectories of ~/.norn/tasks/
Group slug validated: alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores only
TaskTool's create_group and list_groups actions delegate to DiskTaskStore
Test: create_group and list_groups round-trip with tempdir

requirements.title: Wire DiskTaskStore in builder.rs
requirements.spec: norn-cli builder.rs SHALL configure SharedTaskStore to wrap DiskTaskStore as the production implementation.
acceptance: build_runtime constructs a DiskTaskStore with root_dir from paths::norn_dir()/tasks/ and a default group slug derived from the session
SharedTaskStore wrapping DiskTaskStore is available for installation (NA-005 installs it on ToolContext)
RuntimeBundle gains a shared_task_store field or the store is passed through to the extension wiring path
Test: build_runtime with defaults produces a bundle that includes a DiskTaskStore-backed SharedTaskStore

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the TaskStore trait — that was done in NA-003
SHALL NOT install SharedTaskStore on ToolContext — that is NA-005
SHALL NOT add hardcoded limits on task count, group count, or file size (CO1)
SHALL NOT use a database — filesystem only (CO4)
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: cargo test -p norn -p norn-cli — all tests pass
DiskTaskStore CRUD cycle works with tempdir — verified by test
Atomic claim prevents double-claiming — verified by test
Task files survive process restart (read back after write) — verified by test
Named task groups round-trip — verified by test
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
